name: Guardrails AI
tagline: Validate LLM inputs and outputs with composable guards in Python
seoTitle: Guardrails AI - Input/Output Validation for LLMs
metaDescription: Guardrails AI is a Python framework that wraps LLM calls with composable validators to check structure, type, and safety risks before responses reach users.
overview: Guardrails AI is a Python framework for building reliable LLM applications. It runs Input and Output Guards around your model calls to detect, measure, and reduce specific risks, and it can also force model output into a defined structure.

It is aimed at developers who already call an LLM in Python and want a checkpoint before a response reaches a user. You pull pre-built validators from the Guardrails Hub, combine them into a Guard, and let that Guard validate text or generate structured data.

As a guardrail framework, it sits between your application code and the model. Validators cover things like regex matching, competitor mentions, and toxic language, and each Guard decides what to do when a check fails.
features: Pull pre-built validators (regex match, competitor check, toxic language, and more) from the Guardrails Hub
Combine multiple validators into a single Input or Output Guard
Configurable failure handling per validator via OnFailAction (for example, raise an exception)
Generate structured data from LLMs using a Pydantic BaseModel with Guard.for_pydantic
Works with proprietary and open-source LLMs, using function calling or prompt-based schema injection
Run Guardrails as a standalone Flask service with a REST API via guardrails start

gettingStarted.intro: Install the package, configure the Hub CLI, then install a validator and build a Guard.

steps.heading: Install and configure
steps.body: Install the package from PyPI and run the Hub configuration command.

steps.heading: Install a validator from the Hub
steps.body: Pull a validator, such as regex match, from the Guardrails Hub.

steps.heading: Create a Guard and validate
steps.body: Build a Guard from the installed validator and run validation. Passing text returns normally; failing text raises an exception.

useCases: Block or flag toxic language in model responses before they reach end users
Enforce that an LLM does not mention named competitors in generated text
Validate that model output matches an expected format, such as a phone number pattern
Generate structured JSON data from an LLM that conforms to a Pydantic schema
